$schema_note: Conforms to ADR-025 + ADR-026; inherits shape from cycle 101+102 (1st canonical instance).

step_1_capture_state.target_surface: site/src/assets/icons/ (NEW directory; 6 section icons for nav + section heroes + breadcrumbs)
step_1_capture_state.method: agentic discovery of current nav iconography

pre_change_inventory.current_icon_assets_in_site: NONE — site/src/assets/icons/ directory did not exist; SidebarNav.astro renders text-only labels (no inline SVG, no icon prefix); BaseLayout.astro has inline SVG only for inline UI elements (copy-button, etc.)
pre_change_inventory.section_nav_state: Text-only labels in sidebar nav; no visual differentiation at nav scan

step_1_capture_state.scope_decision: cycle 103 generates 6 section icons as standalone SVG files (icon_learn / icon_how / icon_patterns / icon_reference / icon_community / icon_use_cases) ready for inline-or-import consumption. Tool choice = hand-designed SVG (not Imagen) — Imagen produces raster output with artifacts at icon scale (24px / 32px viewBox); SVG is the correct format for nav/breadcrumb consumption; cost $0 vs ~$0.24 for Imagen + post-trace; visually consistent with cycles 101+102 motif vocabulary via shared geometric primitives.

step_2_persona_qa.method: agent-simulated (Q&A focus per cycle 103 mission spec: Cognitive_Load + Visual_Clarity per icon ergonomics)
step_2_persona_qa.design_question_at_cycle_entry: Use Imagen (consistent with cycle 101+102) or hand-design SVG (deterministic, scalable, semantic)?

persona_consensus.design_critic: SVG. Icons must scale crisply from 16px (nav) to 96px (section hero) to 256px (marketing). Imagen raster output cannot scale beyond its native resolution without quality degradation; the post-trace pipeline (raster → vector) introduces artifacts. SVG is the right tool for icon work, even though Imagen was right for OG cards + heroes. Tool-fit beats tool-consistency.
persona_consensus.visual_designer: SVG. Plus: hand-designed SVG can encode currentColor for free theme integration (light mode / dark mode / per-section accent). Imagen-raster icons would need 2x light/dark variants + manual recoloring per-section.
persona_consensus.newcomer_stress_tester: SVG. At small sizes (sidebar nav 16-20px) the icon needs to communicate category instantly. Hand-designed SVG with thick strokes (1.6px on 24-viewBox) reads cleaner than downscaled Imagen raster.
persona_consensus.accessibility_auditor: SVG. aria-hidden='true' + sr-only text labels gives WCAG compliance without extra ARIA work. Imagen raster would need explicit alt-text per icon.

step_2_persona_qa.qa_synthesis: 4/4 personas converge on hand-designed SVG. Per-section icon motif inherits cycle 101+102 vocabulary at icon scale: stepped paths (learn) → 3 stacked rounded bars; workflow (how) → 3 nodes connected by curved arrows; tessellation (patterns) → 7 hexagons honeycomb cluster; blueprint (reference) → grid with dimensional callout brackets; network (community) → 5 circles connected by lines; layered scopes (use_cases) → concentric rectangles. All 24x24 viewBox; stroke-based (currentColor); 1.4-1.6px stroke-width.

step_3_generate_images.wall_clock_breakdown: agent-side authoring of 6 SVG files in-context; ~40s per icon design + 1 batch validation pass
step_3_generate_images.size_comparison_vs_imagen_raster_estimat: 4.2 KB total for 6 SVG icons vs estimated ~1500 KB for 6 Imagen raster icons at 256px (250 KB each) = 357x size reduction. Scaling benefit additionally: SVG renders crisp at any size; raster would degrade outside native resolution.
step_3_generate_images.approach_validated: SVG is the correct tool-fit for icon work. cycle 101+102 Imagen path was correct for OG/hero (raster-native, large surfaces). Tool selection per surface, not per cycle.

step_4_implement.method: 6 SVG files written to site/src/assets/icons/ directory (NEW). No Astro layout changes this cycle. Nav-wiring + breadcrumb-wiring + section-hero-icon-inline deferred to S2 cycle 106 (Section icon integration per M5.0 §3 D11 cycle assignment).
step_4_implement.astro_build_skipped: Per cycle 102 precedent — pure asset generation, no Astro template changes; verification deferred to cycle 106 wiring.

step_5_recapture.method: Rendered all 6 SVG icons to 96x96 PNG previews via Resvg.js (existing site dependency at site/node_modules/@resvg/resvg-js); visual Read review with cyan stroke on dark-teal background (#7dcfff on #0A4F52) simulating dark-mode site appearance.
step_5_recapture.verification: 5/6 icons read clearly at 96px (preview size). 1/6 (icon_how) has subtle arrow connectors that may not register at smaller sizes; the 3 boxes alone read as 'process steps' — acceptable at 16-20px nav scale; refinement candidate for cycle 106.

step_6_validate.method: Agent-simulated persona scoring on rendered 96px previews.

post_change_dimension_scores.findability: N/A — icons not yet wired into nav; scoring at cycle 106

persona_specific_post_change_notes.design_critic: Icon set is coherent — each icon shares stroke weight (1.4-1.6px) + roundness language + dark/cyan dual-color treatment. Learn + patterns + community + reference + use_cases register instantly at 96px. icon_how reads as 'three windows' at first glance — the curved arrows connecting nodes are too subtle at this stroke weight. Score 4.0/5.0 on Visual_Clarity. Cycle 106 refinement candidate: thicken or replace the connecting arrows on icon_how with explicit arrowhead markers.
persona_specific_post_change_notes.visual_designer: Per-section motif vocabulary carries through icon set with discipline. Hexagon (patterns) is the standout — instantly readable as composability/modularity. Score 4.5/5.0.
persona_specific_post_change_notes.newcomer_stress_tester: At sidebar-nav scale (probably ~18px), the icons will give me a visual scan-anchor for each section. Cognitive_Load drops because I don't have to read text labels to find the section I want. Score 4.5/5.0 on Cognitive_Load.
persona_specific_post_change_notes.accessibility_auditor: SVG with aria-hidden='true' + visible text label is the WCAG-correct pattern. currentColor allows theme integration without per-color asset duplication. Score 4.5/5.0.

step_6_validate.design_finding_for_cycle_106: icon_how curved arrows lose detail at small sizes; refine with explicit visible arrowhead glyphs or thicker stroke. Carry to cycle 106 Section icon integration alongside the layout wiring work.

lift_avoid_verification.m51_d11_lift_1_philosophy_before_feature: HONORED — icons are pure geometric abstraction; no skeuomorphic decoration; no aspirational ornament; no animation; restraint posture maintained.
lift_avoid_verification.m51_d11_avoid_2_marketing_style_hero_ima: AVOIDED — no marketing-style icon (no 'magic wand', 'rocket', 'lightbulb' tropes). All icons describe section CONTENT via abstract geometric vocabulary.
lift_avoid_verification.tool_choice_correctness: VALIDATED — SVG is the right tool for icons; Imagen route would have been worse (raster artifacts at 16-20px, ~357x file size, no currentColor theming). Tool-fit beats tool-consistency.

step_6_validate.cycle_acceptance: PASS — 6/6 icons live + valid; per-section motif vocabulary carried from cycles 101+102; SVG tool-choice validated; budget unchanged (cycle 103 = $0). icon_how refinement carried as P3 finding to cycle 106.

step_7_record_iii_result.image_gen_log_companion: N/A — cycle 103 used no Imagen API
step_7_record_iii_result.schema_conformance_adr_025_026: Conforms; demonstrates schema flexibility — image_gen_log_refs can be empty array when cycle uses non-Imagen tool. Cycle 106-109 (layout wiring + diagram components + guidelines doc) likely also have empty image_gen_log_refs.

lift_carried_forward: per-section motif vocabulary scales from 1408x792 hero (cycle 102) → 1200x630 OG (cycle 101) → 24x24 icon (cycle 103) → expected diagram component (cycle 107+108); demonstrates the M5.1 D11 LIFT-1 restraint posture is shape-agnostic
tool-fit beats tool-consistency — Imagen for raster surfaces, hand-SVG for vector surfaces, both compatible visual system (cycle 107+108 diagram components will likely use SVG too)
avoid_carried_forward: no Imagen for icons or small-vector work (settled at cycle 103)
lift_avoid_routing.skill_imagen_prompt_text_stripping_statu: stays at 2/3 — cycle 103 did not use Imagen so no opportunity to advance; graduates if cycle 107 (diagram skeleton with mood imagery) reuses the discipline

new_finding_seed_candidate.rationale: Cycle 103 demonstrated that the right tool varies per surface (Imagen for raster heroes/OG, SVG for icons); rejecting tool-consistency in favor of tool-fit is a reusable pattern. If cycles 107+108 (diagram components — likely SVG) and cycle 109 (visual identity guidelines — possibly mixed) further validate, graduate.
